Explanation: This report presents a well-structured analysis of chromatographic peak symmetry detection using established statistical methods (AsLS baseline correction, USP tailing factor calculations). The methodology is clearly described with three sequential steps appropriately documented. However, the practical value is significantly limited by the null result—zero anomalies detected across all 11 runs. While this negative result is scientifically valid, it reduces the depth of analysis and originality. The report acknowledges this limitation transparently, noting that the visualization framework was successfully tested but represents methodology rather than actual fault detection. Writing quality is professional and technically sound. The included code listings demonstrate technical competence with appropriate error handling and optimization considerations. The single figure shows baseline-corrected chromatograms but lacks detailed statistical summaries (e.g., tailing factor distributions, safety margins). The analysis would be stronger with additional diagnostics exploring why no anomalies were detected or providing metrics on how close measurements came to threshold boundaries. Scientific rigor is adequate but could be enhanced through sensitivity analysis or confidence intervals.
